feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Following messages raised while executing migration of Fixed Assets - Postings
entry.content:
Following messages raised while executing migration of Fixed Assets - Postings

Error: Inform your system administrator (system error) (AA 481)
Warning: Sheet Cumulative Values; Crcy type 10: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
Warning: Sheet Posted Values; Crcy type 10: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
Warning: Sheet Cumulative Values; Crcy type 30: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
Warning: Sheet Posted Values; Crcy type 30: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
The same does not occur while simulating the migration.

entry.title: Following messages raised while executing migration of Fixed Assets - Postings
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

Following messages raised while executing migration of Fixed Assets - Postings

Error: Inform your system administrator (system error) (AA 481)
Warning: Sheet Cumulative Values; Crcy type 10: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
Warning: Sheet Posted Values; Crcy type 10: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
Warning: Sheet Cumulative Values; Crcy type 30: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
Warning: Sheet Posted Values; Crcy type 30: Specify the historical foreign currency values (FAA_POST 510)
The same does not occur while simulating the migration.

entry.title: What is the practical use of Data controller tab in Supplier and Customer LTMC template?
entry.content:
In SAP Cloud Private edition - Migrate your data

What is the practical use of Data controller tab in Supplier and Customer LTMC template? In earlier version we did not have this tab

entry.title: Mapping Tasks Button Disabled in S/4HANA Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are currently in the testing phase of our S/4HANA data migration using the Migration Cockpit.

In one of our projects, the “Mapping Tasks” button is enabled and working as expected. However, when we create a new project with same migration object and complete the “Prepare Mapping Tasks” step, the “Mapping Tasks” button becomes disabled afterward. Due to this, we are unable to access the mapping screen again to review or confirm the mapping values.

This behavior is not consistent across projects, as the button remains enabled in another testing project under similar conditions.

Could you please advise:

  • Under what conditions does the “Mapping Tasks” button become disabled?
  • Is there a configuration, status setting, or authorization that controls this?
  • How can we re-enable the button to review and confirm the mapping values?

Your guidance would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Fixed asset migration for French company
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to ask for support regarding fixed asset migration into SAP S4HANA cloud public edition via migration cockpit for a French company. 

We are migrating an asset which was acquired in February 2020, depreciation start date is 01/02/2020. 

Useful life 4 years:

 

Just to mention, we have 2 ledgers (0L as operational ledger and 2L as local ledger).

The master data was properly migrated. However we are having issues with the second part of migration - the fixed asset postings. In template for fixed asset-postings we have the posting key tab fulfilled as follows:

And the cumulative values tab:

With this file being simulated, we are receiving an error messages for 2L ledger (0L is successfully simulated):

-depreciation area 93 does not manage APC values

-acquisition value will become negative in depreciation area 93

I do not understand, how could the acquisition value become negative, if the acquisition cost is 848,00 and depreciation 847,99. There should be 1 cent left. 

This is the fixed asset in system:

Legacy data transfer settings:

And migration key date is 31.12.2025

The 92 and 93 depreciation areas are specific for companies operating in FR. Is there something wrong with the set-up? What should we change to be able to migrate the asset? 

Is there anyone who has experience with FR fixed asset migration?

Thanks in advance

Zuzana Gersiova

entry.title: Transfer objects created in Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO. (Message-type and data-type)
entry.content:
Hi everyone !

    I'm working on a project migrating SAP PI/PO to Cloud Integration NEO (the migration from NEO to Cloud Foundry will be next year). To perform the migration using the migration tool, following the instructions provided by SAP, we created a BTP Trial account and imported all the objects from PI/PO. After the migration, I'm transferring the objects and iFlows to BTP NEO (Cloud Integration). However, Cloud Integration NEO doesn't have the option to import Data-Types or Message-Types that were created during the migration in the Integration Suite of the Trial account. My question is: has anyone done this transfer from Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO before? How do I transfer the data-types and message-types to NEO? And if I have to create them manually in NEO, how do I create these objects in their iFlows on Cloud Integration NEO ?

entry.title: SLT System Migration – SQL Server on Windows 2019 to SAP HANA on RHEL | Best Approach?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working on a system migration for our SAP Landscape Transformation (SLT) environment and would appreciate guidance from those who've done something similar.

Current Setup:

  • SLT source DB: Microsoft SQL Server running on Windows Server 2019

Target Setup:

  • Database: SAP HANA
  • OS: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL)

Background: This migration is part of a broader initiative — once the SLT system is on HANA, it will be used to support a BDC (Business Data Cloud) scenario downstream.

My Question: What is the recommended approach for this kind of heterogeneous system migration (OS change + DB change)?

Specifically:

  • Can SUM (Software Update Manager) handle this type of migration (DB + OS switch)?
  • Is SWPM (Software Provisioning Manager) the right tool here?
  • Or is there a completely different recommended path (e.g., System Copy + DB migration using HANA MDC)?

Any pointers to relevant SAP Notes, guides, or real-world experience would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: 1ZQ stock migration
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We are using scope item 1ZQ, and we want to migrate stock to an external WMS.

We tried to used the migration template "Material Inventory Balance" but we have an error message M7 491 "Inventory Balance Migration into WM/EWM storage location & is not possible"

We found the following SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/0002789369 that indicates "Check the configuration environment of the WM/EWM storage location."

Can you give us more details about what to check in the configuration environment ?

Thanks in advance for your help.

Eric

entry.title: Standard Template for Bulk Upload of Adjusting Entries in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I hope you are doing well.

I would like your guidance regarding the standard method for uploading bulk adjusting journal entries in SAP S/4HANA.

  1. Could you please confirm the exact standard template name used for bulk upload of journal entries (e.g., via Migration Cockpit, LTMC, or any other tool)?
  2. What is the exact path or transaction to download this template, especially when working in QAS environment?
  3. Is the same template used for uploading adjusting entries, or is there a separate template specifically for opening balances / migration?
  4. Can this upload process be used only for Income & Expense GL accounts, or can it also handle:
    • CWIP (Capital Work in Progress) entries
    • DWIP (Development Work in Progress) entries

I would highly appreciate your expert guidance on best practices for this process.

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Ammad

entry.title: SAP ECC to S4 HANA data migration
entry.content: whilst working on SAP ECC to S4 HANA  migration there is a requirement of new ledgers being introduced in S4 HANA , there are 3 ledgers in SAP ECC and 8 Ledgers in SAP S4 HANA what is the tool provided by SAP to derive the additional 5 new ledger data in S4 HANA

entry.title: Error during Mass Maintenance: "Business Partner with GUID ... already exists"
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working on mass-updating Business Partners (BP) using the "Manage Mass Maintenance" app to set them to inactive status.

Context:

  1. I exported the current BP master data using "Export Master Data - Business Partners".

  2. In the file, I mapped the following columns: SOURCE_ID, PARTNER, XBLCK, NAME_ORG1, NAME_ORG2, NAME_ORG3, VALID_FROM, and VALID_TO.

  3. To deactivate the BPs, I set the XBLCK field to 'X' and uploaded the file back into the "Manage Mass Maintenance - Business Partner" process.

Issue:
Instead of updating the existing records, the system returns the following error for many BPs:

"Business Partner with GUID [GUID_VALUE] already exists"

It seems the system is trying to create a new BP record instead of performing an update/change on the existing one, despite the PARTNER number being included in the input file.

Questions:

  1. Why does the system treat an update request as an attempt to create a duplicate record with the same GUID?

  2. Is there a specific configuration or an additional field (e.g., action code or specific indicator) required to explicitly instruct the system to "Update" rather than "Create"?

  3. What are the mandatory columns and their specific values required to perform an "Inactive" status update for existing BPs in the "Manage Mass Maintenance" app? (For example, is XBLCK alone sufficient, or do I need to include specific validity dates or action indicators?)

Any guidance on the correct procedure for mass-deactivating existing BPs via this app would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: HANA Cockpit retention not deleting old SYSTEMDB backup generations although log_mode is normal
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am testing HANA Cockpit backup retention for SYSTEMDB@HSD and would like to clarify if the behavior I am seeing is expected.

Configuration:

  • Backup destination: File
  • Backup path: /hana/shared/HSD/HDB00/backup/data/SYSTEMDB/
  • Daily complete data backup: around 02:00 AM
  • Automatically Delete Backup Generations: Yes
  • Deletion option: Remove from backup catalog and delete physically from file system
  • Retain Backup Generations Younger Than: 1 day
  • Minimum No. of Retained Backup Generations: 1
  • Start Daily Automatic Deletion: 9:00 PM UTC
  • Assigned Database User: SYSTEM
  • log_mode = normal

On the morning of Apr 28, I still saw these complete data backup generations in both the backup catalog and file system:

2026-04-26 02:00
2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00

However, when I checked again on Apr 29, the Apr 26 backup generation had been deleted, and the remaining backup files were:

2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00
2026-04-29 02:00

So the retention policy seems to be working, but not exactly as I initially expected.

My questions are:

  1. Is this behavior expected for Retain Backup Generations Younger Than = 1 day?
  2. Does HANA Cockpit retention evaluate and delete old backup generations only once per daily scheduler run, instead of deleting them immediately when they become older than 1 day?
  3. Why would the Apr 26 generation still exist on Apr 28 but be deleted on Apr 29?
  4. Is the correct interpretation that HANA deletes complete backup generations based on the scheduler run and generation eligibility, not simply based on the file date?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Guidance Needed: Migrating Mutual Fund Investments from FI-AA (Asset Accounting) to SAP TRM (Treasur
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am looking for expert advice regarding a module transition. Currently, our company manages Mutual Fund investments as Fixed Assets (FI-AA). We have now configured the Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) module and want to move these investments there for better management.

Our current process is simple: we only record the Purchase and Retirement of these funds in SAP.

My Proposed Approach

Since we do not have Fiori apps available in our landscape, I am planning the following:

Asset Retirement: Retire all existing Mutual Fund assets in FI-AA to clear the Asset sub-ledger.

Data Migration: Use LSMW to bring the data into the Treasury module.

I need clarification on the following points:

Migration Object: What exactly should be migrated into TRM? Should I migrate the original historical posting documents, or just the current "Open Position" (quantity and value) as of the cut-over date?

Standard Uploaders: Aside from LSMW, is there a standard SAP technical uploader (like a BAPI or a specific functional transaction) designed to initialize positions in the Transaction Manager?

Reconciliation: Since we only handle purchases and retirements, what is the best way to ensure the GL stays balanced during this shift? For example, if I retire the asset to a "Clearing Account," should I then use that same clearing account for the "Purchase" entry in TRM?

Implementation Steps: Could someone provide a brief sequence of steps for this "Fixed Asset to TRM" move? I want to ensure I don't miss any critical integration settings between the two sub-ledgers.

A Simple Example: If we own 1,000 units of a Mutual Fund currently sitting in Asset Accounting, would the correct flow be to retire those 1,000 units in FI-AA today and "buy" them into TRM at the same cost price tomorrow to maintain continuity?

I would greatly appreciate any guidance or documentation from anyone who has handled a similar "Asset-to-Treasury" migration.

entry.title: Migration Cockpit: Stuck in "preparation" for migration project
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

Anomalous behavior occurs in the Migration Cockpit when creating and preparing migration projects.
When you create a project, the "Migration Project Preparation" activity  remains in  a "Scheduled" state  for an extended time without advancing to execution or completion.

 

I can create the Migration Project for staging tables without a problem, but it gets stuck in the preparation of the project, before I can download the template

 

 

I don't get jobs or something to stop the execution and creating new projects with fewer migration objects has not worked for us

Example 1

 

 

Example 2

 

I would greatly appreciate your support

entry.title: UII Not Populating During LTMC Inventory Upload
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on Inventory upload through LTMC. Serial Number is a standard field and it is getting populated successfully during posting.

We tried changing the field mapping to UID/UII in LTMOM, but the UII field is still not getting populated.

Our colleagues have created custom fields in the Fiori Custom Fields app using Business Context for other templates like Equipment and Notification and it was working fine.

Need clarification on:

  • What is the correct Business Context for Inventory Upload template / Goods Movement template?
  • Is UII supported through standard LTMC mapping?
  • Is there any other approach available for populating UII during inventory posting via LTMC Template?

Any suggestions would be helpful.

Thanks.

entry.title: Mass Copy of BOM and Routing to New Plant in S/4HANA Public Cloud (Bulk ~500 Materials)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working on extending existing materials to a new plant in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

I have around 500 finished materials for which the following already exists in the old plant:

  • Bill of Material (BOM)
  • Routing

Now, I want to replicate the same setup for a new plant, ensuring:

  • Same BOM structure (header + items + alternatives if any)
  • Same Routing (operations, new work centers.)

Is there any standard way in Public Cloud to:

  1. Copy BOMs from one plant to another in bulk
  2. Copy Routings from one plant to another in bulk

What I Checked So Far

  • Migration object (BOM / Routing) → seems possible, but requires extraction + upload again
  • Fiori apps → mostly support single material

entry.title: Migration fixed asset nbv less than 1
entry.content:
Hi,
I would like to inquire about the correct approach to migrate a fixed asset where the Net Book Value (NBV) is less than 1. Below are the details for reference:

Cost of Asset: 100,000
Accumulated Depreciation: 99,999.50
NBV: 0.50

When attempting to migrate this asset, the following error messages are encountered:

"The fixed asset in depreciation area 01 is below the cutoff value in the amount of 1."
"The fixed asset in depreciation area 15 is below the cutoff value in the amount of 1."

Could you please advise on how to resolve this issue? Any guidance would be greatly appreciated."

entry.title: Migration Cockpit - Direct Approach : "MM - Material Consumption" : Re-Select is not possible
entry.content:
Scenario : Migration Cockpit - Direct Approach

Migration Object : MM - Material  Consumption

Steps :

  • Create a new migration object : MM - Material  Consumption
  • Run : "Select Data" --> result is as expected
  • Run again "Select Data" --> instances are removed, but no new instances are added 

In the messages I see an error, showing only a database table.

 

The mentioned table still contains data, which I suspect is responsible for the message: "x instances ignored due to duplicate".

Thanks sharing your ideas,

Hans

entry.title: Migration Cockpit - Direct Approach - "PS - Project" - How to migrate Custom Fields ?
entry.content:
Scenario :

  • Migration Cockpit - Direct Approach
  • Migration Object : PS - Project

Requirement : we have custom fields in the table PROJ. How can we change the migration object "PS - Project", to include the custom fields ?

The migration object is based on the Function Module "CNV_OT_APPL_PE_S4_WRAP_PS". 

This Function Module does not have input parameters as EXTENSIONIN and EXTENSIONOUT.

Thanks for sharing your ideas.

Hans

entry.title: Material BOM Migration (2602) - BOM Item Document Assignment - Error 29 356 although complete docume
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am currently migrating Material BOMs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Release 2602) using the Migration Cockpit. 
I am trying to migrate a BOM item with Item Category = D (Document).

Issue Description

The referenced Document Info Record (DIR) already exists in the system and can be assigned manually to the BOM without any issue. 
However, during the Migration Simulation, the process fails with the following error:

  • Message Class: 29
  • Message Number: 356
  • "Please enter complete document key"

Document Information

The document exists in the system with the following key:

  • Document Type: ZBO
  • Document Number: 10000000003
  • Document Version: A
  • Document Part: 000
  • Status: H3 (Released)

Migration Template Details

BOM Item

  • BOM Usage = 1
  • Alternative BOM = 2
  • Item ID = 00000002
  • Item Category = D

BOM Item Document Assignment

The following fields are maintained:

  • Material Number
  • Plant
  • BOM Usage
  • Alternative BOM
  • Item ID = 00000002
  • Document Type = ZBO
  • Document Number = 10000000003
  • Document Version = A
  • Document Part = 000

Verification Already Performed

  • The Document Info Record exists in the system.
  • The document status is valid (H3 – Released).
  • Manual assignment of the same document to the BOM works successfully.
  • All document key fields are maintained in the BOM Item Document Assignment worksheet.
  • The Item ID in the BOM Item sheet matches the Item ID in the BOM Item Document Assignment sheet.

Question

Has anyone successfully migrated BOM document assignments (Item Category D) using the Material BOM migration object in Release 2602?

Are there any:

  • known restrictions,
  • additional mandatory fields,
  • formatting requirements,
  • or linkage dependencies

between the BOM Item and BOM Item Document Assignment worksheets that could trigger this error?

Since the document exists and can be assigned manually, I am wondering whether there is a specific requirement or limitation related to the migration object itself.

Any feedback or experience on this topic would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best Regards.
